notes.text:
An update is available for php.
This update affects Rocky Linux 10.
A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE list
notes.title: Topic

notes.text:
PHP is an HTML-embedded scripting language commonly used with the Apache HTTP Server.

Security Fix(es):

* PHP: PHP: Denial of Service via improper handling of signed characters in ctype functions (CVE-2026-7258)

* PHP: PHP-FPM: PHP-FPM: Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability via improper URL sanitation (CVE-2026-6735)

* php: NULL pointer dereference in php_mb_check_encoding() via mb_ereg_search_init() (CVE-2026-7259)

* php: NULL pointer dereference in SOAP apache:Map decoder with missing <value> (CVE-2026-7262)

* php: signed integer overflow in metaphone() (CVE-2026-7568)

For more details about the security issue(s), including the impact, a CVSS score, acknowledgments, and other related information, refer to the CVE page(s) listed in the References section.

This advisory is provided by Ctrl IQ, Inc for Rocky Linux systems. For more information about CIQ security practices, visit https://ciq.com/security.
notes.title: Description

references.summary: Red Hat CSAF document for RHSA-2026:23388
